definition.en: For Seth, Framework 1 is simply a term representing the everyday, linear, conscious 'working reality' we take for granted, the one in which 'time' and events automatically unfold in moment after undeniable moment.
definition.zh: 对赛斯而言，架构一只是一个术语，代表我们视为理所当然的日常、线性、有意识的‘运作实相’，在其中，‘时间’与事件在无可否认的片刻接续中自动展开。

definition.en: It's the milieu in which most of us unthinkingly live out our physical lives.
definition.zh: 它是我们大多数人毫不思索地度过物质生活的环境。

definition.en: Such events, however, consist of indivisible 'particles' and faster-than-light perceptions1 that never show. In other words, they contain psychic components that flow from Framework 2 into Framework 1.
definition.zh: 然而，这类事件由不可分割的‘粒子’与从未显现的超光速感知组成。换言之，它们包含从架构二流入架构一的心灵成分。

definition.en: Each child's birth changes the world, obviously, for it sets up an instant psychological momentum that begins to affect action in Framework 1 and Framework 2 alike.
definition.zh: 显然，每个孩子的出生都改变了世界，因为它建立起一种即时的心理动量，开始同样影响架构一与架构二中的行动。

explanation: 架构一是指我们日常线性、有意识的“工作现实”，即我们习以为常的世界。在这个框架中，时间以连续不断的时刻自动展开，事件遵循因果律依次发生。这是大多数人无意识度过物理生活的环境。架构一中的自我（ego）专门处理这个环境，它依据因果规则和连续时刻运作，应对客观化的现实，主要目的是处理“效果”世界，即已经发生的事件。在这个框架里，我们相信能量有限、会耗尽，努力才能产生结果。架构一就像只学会了写“看汤姆跑”这样的简单句子，思维局限于对物体的简单感知，缺乏想象力。它依赖演绎推理，并不断用物理事件的表面经验来验证自己的结果。

quotes.text: For Seth, Framework 1 is simply a term representing the everyday, linear, conscious "working reality" we take for granted, the one in which "time" and events automatically unfold in moment after undeniable moment. It's the milieu in which most of us unthinkingly live out our physical lives. Beyond Framework 1, however, exists Framework 2, and it represents the great timeless or simultaneous spacious present that's so dearly a manifestation of All That Is. All of our dreams, plans, thoughts, actions, and choices live in Framework 2; all flow from Framework 2 into Framework 1 according to our beliefs.
quotes.translation: 对赛斯而言，框架一只是一个术语，代表我们视为理所当然的日常、线性、有意识的"运作实相"，在这个实相中，"时间"和事件在不可否认的连续瞬间中自动展开。它是我们大多数人无意识地度过物质生活的环境。然而，在框架一之外，存在着框架二，它代表着那伟大的无时间性或同时性的广阔当下，如此珍贵地展现了一切万有。我们所有的梦想、计划、思想、行动和选择都存在于框架二中；根据我们的信念，一切从框架二流入框架一。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Jung's collective unconscious was an attempt to give your world its psychological roots, but Jung1 could not perceive the clarity, organization, and deeper context in which that collective unconscious has its own existence. Reality as Framework 2 is organized in a different fashion than it is in the Framework 1 world, and the processes of reasoning are far quicker. In Framework 1 the reasoning processes work largely by deduction, and they must constantly check their own results against the seemingly concrete experience of physical events. The reasoning of the inner ego is involved with the creative invention of those experiences. It is involved with events in a context of a different kind, for it deals intimately with probabilities.
quotes.translation: 荣格的集体无意识试图为你们的世界赋予心理根源，但荣格无法看清那个集体无意识自身存在其中的清晰性、组织性和更深层背景。作为框架二的实相，其组织方式与框架一世界不同，推理过程也快得多。在框架一中，推理过程主要依靠演绎，它们必须不断根据看似具体的物理事件经验来检验自身结果。内在自我的推理则涉及对这些经验的创造性发明。它以一种不同的背景处理事件，因为它与可能性密切打交道。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: We will call the world as you physically experience it, Framework 1. In Framework 1, you watch television programs, for example. You have your choice of many channels. You have favorite programs. You follow certain scenes or actors. You watch all of these dramas, hardly understanding how it is that they appear on your screen to begin with. You are certain, however, that if you do buy a television set it will perform in an adequate fashion, whether or not you are familiar with electronics. Period.
quotes.translation: 我们将你们在物质上体验的世界称为框架一。例如，在框架一中，你们看电视节目。你们可以选择许多频道。你们有喜欢的节目。你们追随某些场景或演员。你们观看所有这些戏剧，却几乎不明白它们最初是如何出现在屏幕上的。然而，你们确信，如果你们确实买了一台电视机，它将以适当的方式运作，无论你们是否熟悉电子学。句号。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 815, DECEMBER 17, 1977
9:22 P.M. SATURDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: As you have an ego, fully conscious, directed toward the physical world, you also have what I call an inner ego, directed toward inner reality. You have, in other words, a portion of yourself that is fully conscious in Framework 2. The ego in your ordinary world, which again we will call Framework 1, is uniquely equipped to deal with that environment. It manipulates with rules of cause and effect and consecutive moments. It deals with an objectified reality. It can stretch its capacities, becoming far more aware of inner events than it is normally allowed to do, but its main purpose is to deal with the world of effects, to encounter events.
quotes.translation: 正如你们有一个完全有意识、面向物质世界的自我，你们也有我所说的内在自我，面向内在实相。换句话说，你们有一部分自己在框架二中是完全有意识的。你们普通世界中的自我——我们再次称之为框架一——具有独特的能力来处理那个环境。它按照因果法则和连续瞬间来运作。它处理客观化的实相。它可以扩展其能力，变得比通常允许的更清楚地意识到内在事件，但其主要目的是处理效果的世界，遭遇事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978
9:27 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Your Framework 1 life is, again, based on the idea that you have only so much energy, that you will wear out, and that a certain expenditure of energy will produce a given amount of work -- in other words, that applied effort of a certain kind will produce the best results. In the same way, it is believed that the energy of the universe will die out. All of this presupposes "the fact" that no new energy is inserted into the world. The source of the world would therefore seem no longer to exist, having worn itself out in the effort to produce physical phenomena. In the light of such thinking, Framework 2 would be an impossibility.
quotes.translation: 你们的框架一生活再次基于这样的观念：你们只有这么多能量，你们会耗尽，一定量的能量消耗会产生一定量的工作——换句话说，某种类型的应用努力会产生最佳结果。同样，人们相信宇宙的能量会消亡。所有这些都预设了"事实"：没有新能量被注入世界。因此，世界的源头似乎不再存在，在努力产生物质现象的过程中耗尽了自身。在这种思维下，框架二是不可能的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 819, which was held last Saturday evening, had nothing to do with Mass Events. In our opinion tonight's session did, however, and for a number of reasons that will become obvious as it's read. Yet Seth didn't call it dictation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Again, the writer or the artist also brings more into his work than the simple ability to write or paint. In one way or another all of his experience is involved. When you pay attention to Framework 1 primarily, it is as if you have learned to write simple sentences with one word neatly before the other. You have not really learned true expression. In your life you are writing sentences like "See Tommy run." Your mind is not really dealing with concepts but with the simple perception of objects, so that little imagination is involved. You can express the location of objects in space, and you can communicate to others in a similar fashion, confirming the physical, obvious properties that others also perceive.
quotes.translation: 再次，作家或艺术家带入其作品中的，也远不止简单的写作或绘画能力。无论以何种方式，他所有的经验都参与其中。当你们主要关注框架一时，就好像你们学会了写简单的句子，一个词整齐地排在另一个词前面。你们并没有真正学会真正的表达。在你们的生活中，你们在写像"看汤米跑"这样的句子。你们的心智并没有真正处理概念，而是处理对物体的简单感知，因此很少涉及想象力。你们可以表达物体在空间中的位置，并以类似的方式与他人交流，确认他人也感知到的物理、明显的属性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 819, which was held last Saturday evening, had nothing to do with Mass Events. In our opinion tonight's session did, however, and for a number of reasons that will become obvious as it's read. Yet Seth didn't call it dictation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I am speaking of that framework now only as it applies to your world -- not in its relationship to other realities. Earlier in his own experience Ruburt described that framework (in Psychic Politics) as the heroic dimension. He saw quite correctly that there was a great give-and-take between the two frameworks -- your regular working one, Framework 1, and this other more comprehensive reality. He did not thoroughly understand, however, the creative ramifications involved, for it did not occur to him at the time that the prime work of your world was actually done by you in that other wider aspect of your existence.
quotes.translation: 我现在谈论这个架构，仅就其适用于你们世界的部分而言——而非它与其他实相的关系。鲁柏早先在他的个人经验中（在《心灵政治》里）将这个架构描述为英雄维度。他相当正确地看到两个架构之间存在着巨大的互动——你们日常运作的架构一，以及这个更全面的另一实相。然而他并未完全理解其中涉及的创造性衍生，因为当时他未曾想到，你们世界的主要工作实际上是由你们在存在的那个更广阔面向中完成的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 822, FEBRUARY 22, 1978
9:27 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Whatever the initial course of action agreed to in just this probable reality by everyone involved, from whatever point in the "past," in Framework 1 the participants have subjected it to an almost infinite variety of choices and modifications through the years: but always--always--within nature's great structure, and accompanied by the utter freedom of each person concerned to accept, reject, abort, or change the whole affair from their individual perspective at any moment....
quotes.translation: 无论最初所有相关者在某个可能实相中——从"过去"的某个时间点——共同商定的行动方针为何，在架构一中，参与者多年来已使其经历近乎无限的变数与修正：但始终——始终——在大自然的宏伟结构之内，且伴随着每个相关者从自身视角在任何时刻接受、拒绝、中止或改变整件事的绝对自由……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: I said that the inner ego reasons, but its reasoning is not restricted to the cause-and-effect limitations that you apply to the reasoning process. The action of the inner ego within the wider sphere of Framework 2 explains many events and seeming coincidences that otherwise seem to make no sense within your world. Many realities within Framework 2 cannot suitably be explained as facts to you in Framework 1, simply because they involve psychological thicknesses that cannot be translated into facts as you think of them. These often appear in the symbolic language of the arts instead, and many of your dreams are translations in which the events of Framework 2 appear in symbolic form.
quotes.translation: 我曾说过内在自我会推理，但其推理不受你们加诸推理过程的因果限制。内在自我在架构二更广阔领域中的运作，解释了许多在你们世界看似毫无意义的事件与巧合。架构二中的许多实相无法作为事实向架构一中的你们恰当解释，只因它们涉及无法转化为你们所认知事实的心理厚度。这些实相往往以艺术的象征语言呈现，而你们的许多梦境正是架构二事件以象征形式出现的翻译。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Even your concepts of creativity are necessarily influenced by Framework 1 thinking, of course, so our sessions do indeed follow a larger pattern than that, giving you certain perspectives from different angles in book dictation, and in other material. To some extent the larger creative pattern of the material, which does exist and is sensed, is nevertheless not directly perceived, for you are bound to perceive it piecemeal.
quotes.translation: 当然，就连你们对创造力的概念也必然受架构一思维影响，因此我们的课程确实遵循着比这更宏大的模式——在书籍口述与其他资料中，从不同角度为你们提供某些视角。那个确实存在且能被感知的、资料本身的更大创造性模式，在某种程度上仍无法被直接觉察，因为你们注定只能零碎地感知它。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 819, which was held last Saturday evening, had nothing to do with Mass Events. In our opinion tonight's session did, however, and for a number of reasons that will become obvious as it's read. Yet Seth didn't call it dictation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: In the same way you experience a birthday party, an automobile accident, a bridge game, or any psychological event as psychologically solid, with a smooth experienced surface that holds together in space and time. Such events, however, consist of indivisible "particles" and faster-than-light perceptions1 that never show. In other words, they contain psychic components that flow from Framework 2 into Framework 1.
quotes.translation: 同样地，你们体验生日派对、车祸、桥牌游戏或任何心理事件时，都将其视为心理上的实体，具有在时空里连贯的平滑经验表面。然而这类事件由不可分割的"粒子"与从未显现的超光速感知构成。换言之，它们包含着从架构二流入架构一的心灵成分。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 826, MARCH 8, 1978
9:35 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Each child's birth changes the world, obviously, for it sets up an instant psychological momentum that begins to affect action in Framework 1 and Framework 2 alike.
quotes.translation: 每个孩子的出生显然都改变了世界，因为它立即建立起一种心理动量，开始同时影响架构一与架构二中的行动。
